what_it_does: This file is a thin, fail-loud TypeScript client for the surface application. It defines the data types that mirror the backend projection contract and exports small fetch helpers that call /api endpoints for projections, context bundles, territory/source records, and embedding-layer metadata.
observations: Defines projection-related types: ProjPoint, Sector, Edge, BindingRef, Projection, ContextBundle, Territory.
Includes extensive inline comments that prescribe rendering rules (operator-law, registry-true, declared-first, humanized-id fallback).
ProjPoint carries optional human-readable fields such as kind_name, kind_meaning, and sector_name alongside machine ids.
Projection.binding includes multi-layer model fields: emb (embedder layer), res (MRL resolution), and quant (binary/full float).
ContextBundle and Territory use index signatures and unknown fields to stay loose and read whatever the bridge returns.
getJSON reads the response as text first, then fails loudly on network errors, non-OK status, and bad JSON.
fetchProjection builds a query string while filtering out undefined and empty-string values.
fetchTerritory is described as the backing for the V's Source verb.
fetchLayers and fetchLayerDims expose registry-true embedder layer and dimension discovery.
standouts: The comments encode strong design rules (operator-law, registry-true) directly inside the type definitions.
getJSON is intentionally loud: network, HTTP, and JSON errors are all surfaced as ApiError.
The type system is used to enforce human-readable rendering through optional name/meaning fields.
conventions: Fail-loud error handling with no silent failures.
Thin client: no business logic, just transport and typing.
Registry-true / non-hardcoded values surfaced via dedicated endpoints.
Optional human-readable fields shadow machine ids.
Loose types with index signatures for context/territory data.
concerns: ContextBundle and Territory index signatures allow any extra property, which can hide misspelled field access at compile time.
The heavy inline policy comments may drift from the actual backend contract over time.
Several fields are typed as unknown (ts, separation, nucleation, identity, corpus_content, corpus_record), leaving consumers to validate at runtime.
notes: This is the surface's typed boundary to the backend: it says what shapes cross the bridge and insists that the UI render human names, not raw ids.
questions: What concrete shape do separation and nucleation take in a Projection response?
What does ts: unknown represent and how should it be formatted for display?
How does the bridge format error messages in non-OK responses?
What is the exact relationship between binding.id and the bindings list?
What are the valid values and semantics for Projection.binding.space and types_space?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a thin, fail-loud client whose types mirror the verified projection contract, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: The file is trying to keep the UI surface decoupled from backend internals by encoding the contract in TypeScript types, while enforcing that operators always see humanized names and meanings rather than raw ids or technical jargon. It also signals support for pluggable embedding layers, resolutions, and quantization modes.
novelty: The projection types embed explicit humanization hooks (kind_name, kind_meaning, sector_name) and are accompanied by prescriptive operator-law comments that turn type definitions into rendering policy. The layer/resolution discovery endpoints are also a distinct multi-model concern.
contribution: This file contributes the typed API boundary and transport functions that the renderer consumes. It defines the projection data shape, humanization fields, and discovery endpoints for embedding layers and dimensions, giving the rest of the surface a stable, fail-loud interface to the backend.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript API client and projection type contract for a thin surface that fetches JSON from /api endpoints and surfaces transport and parse errors loudly.
intention_for_embedding: To give the renderer a stable, typed, humanization-aware interface to the backend projection, context, territory, and embedding-layer endpoints.

symbols.does: Defines the shape of a single projected point returned by the projection endpoint.
symbols.description: ProjPoint describes a polar-layout item with sequence, kind, sector, theta, radius, depth, cell address, summary, timestamps, phases, and many optional analytical fields. It also carries optional human-readable kind_name, kind_meaning, and sector_name so the UI can render names instead of machine ids.

symbols.does: Defines a sector arc in the projection.
symbols.description: Sector is a simple type with an id, human label, optional meaning, and angular from/to bounds. It represents a slice or grouping in the polar layout.

symbols.does: Defines a directed or bidirectional link between two projected points.
symbols.description: Edge connects two point sequence numbers with an optional bidir flag, representing relationships in the projection graph.

symbols.does: Defines a lightweight reference to a binding.
symbols.description: BindingRef contains an id and a human label, used in the Projection.bindings list to let the surface switch or reference active bindings.

symbols.does: Defines the full response shape for the projection endpoint.
symbols.description: Projection bundles metadata (center, now, n, lock, rings, grid, count), the active binding with its configuration and optional meta meaning, a list of binding references, sectors, edges, optional separation/nucleation/scale data, and the array of ProjPoint items.

symbols.does: Defines the loose response shape for the scored context endpoint.
symbols.description: ContextBundle holds an address, an optional list of scored context items, a more count, an optional error, and an index signature so the surface can read whatever extra fields the bridge returns.

symbols.does: Defines the loose response shape for the territory/source endpoint.
symbols.description: Territory carries the resolved record behind an address, including scheme, identity, corpus content/record, relations, notes, operator directions, and leg flags, plus an index signature for additional bridge fields.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::getJSON | function | async function getJSON<T>(url: string): Promise<T> {
symbols.does: Generic fetch helper that retrieves JSON and fails loudly on network, HTTP, or parse errors.
symbols.description: getJSON uses fetch with an Accept header, reads the response body as text, throws ApiError on network failure or non-OK status (including the response body snippet), and then parses the text as JSON, throwing again if parsing fails.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::ApiError | class | export class ApiError extends Error {}
symbols.does: Custom error class used to surface API failures.
symbols.description: ApiError extends the built-in Error class and acts as a typed carrier for network, HTTP, and JSON parse failures from the API client.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::fetchProjection | function | export function fetchProjection(params: Record<string, string | number | undefined>): Promise<Projection> {
symbols.does: Fetches a projection from /api/projection with query parameters.
symbols.description: fetchProjection builds a query string from the supplied params, dropping undefined and empty-string values, then calls getJSON to retrieve a Projection.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::fetchContext | function | export function fetchContext(address: string): Promise<ContextBundle> {
symbols.does: Fetches the scored context bundle for an address.
symbols.description: fetchContext calls getJSON on /api/context with the encoded address and returns a ContextBundle.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::fetchTerritory | function | export function fetchTerritory(address: string): Promise<Territory> {
symbols.does: Fetches the structured territory/source record for an address.
symbols.description: fetchTerritory calls getJSON on /api/territory with the encoded address and returns a Territory, backing the Source verb.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::fetchLayers | function | export function fetchLayers(): Promise<Record<string, string[]>> {
symbols.does: Discovers the available embedder layers per space.
symbols.description: fetchLayers calls getJSON on /api/layers and returns a mapping from space to available embedder layer names, used to populate the layer picker.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/lib/api.ts::fetchLayerDims | function | export function fetchLayerDims(): Promise<Record<string, Record<string, number>>> {
symbols.does: Discovers the full vector dimension for each embedder layer.
symbols.description: fetchLayerDims calls getJSON on /api/layer-dims and returns a nested mapping of space to layer to dimension, used to derive the MRL resolution ladder.
